mnemonics: Normal Forms Are Like Goldilocks: 1NF (just right data grouping), 2NF (no partial dependencies), 3NF (no transitive dependencies), BCNF (super strict on determinants)
keyPoints: Functional Dependencies define relationships between attributes, forming the basis for normalization.
Normalization is the process of organizing data to reduce redundancy and improve integrity.
Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) is a stronger version of 3NF where every determinant must be a candidate key.
commonMistakes: Assuming normalization always improves performance; over-normalization can lead to excessive joins and slower queries.
Creating a table without a proper primary key or candidate key, leading to insertion, deletion, and update anomalies.
